_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Localized varicose veins with competent greater saphenous vein
Initial treatment option for incompetence of greater saphenous vein & perforator vein before considering more invasive surgery or deep venous reconstruction

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with details of clinical examination and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Was the indication for the surgery mentioned?
Was the duplex scan report submitted?
Is there evidence of non-surgical/medical management tried but failed?

common_queries: Indication for the procedure (surgery) not mentioned at pre-authorization
Duplex scan report not submitted at pre-authorization
No evidence of non-surgical/medical management tried but failed at pre-authorization
Detailed procedure notes not submitted at claim stage
Detailed Discharge Summary not submitted at claim stage
Clinical notes with detailed history, signs and symptoms, clinical examination, planned line of treatment, and indications for the procedure are missing or incomplete
Clinical photographs (pre-op or post-op) not submitted

clinical_key_pointers: Varicose veins are the most common vein disorder.
Varicose veins are dilated, tortuous veins caused by dilation of normal veins due to increased venous pressure.
They are visible surface manifestations of an underlying syndrome of venous insufficiency.
They occur in varying severity and different clinical presentations.
2% of these patients present with skin changes and morbid venous ulcers.
